briefing.headline: Rig up a Swim Jig — finesse pre-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Swim Jig is a strong choice for pre-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better act
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Stop the bait mid-cast and let it fall back toward bottom to increase catch rate by 20-30% — trailing bass can't resist a shad imitator dropping from the school like a dying shad
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'For skip casting, use the heaviest line you can get away with — heavier line makes line management easier. 50lb braid for frogs and swim jigs in colored water. 20lb fluorocarbon for sparser cover or clearer water. 14lb fluoro for finesse jig...
Hackney: 'Uses braid even in clear water when swimming jigs. As long as it is a moving bait, line type doesn't seem to matter — bass chomp regardless.'

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
